agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A production logistics system enforces a strict one-Carrier-per-Shipment invariant with supervisor-only reassignment. The product team requests an auto-split feature that would allow shipments over 500 kg to be divided among multiple carriers without supervisor involvement, directly conflicting with two established domain rules.","bounded_context":"Shipment Fulfillment / Carrier Assignment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of freight to be delivered, which must have exactly one assigned Carrier at all times from assignment through dispatch."},{"term":"Carrier","definition":"The logistics provider assigned to transport a Shipment."},{"term":"Sub-shipment","definition":"A proposed new concept: a child Shipment created by splitting a parent Shipment that exceeds a weight threshold. Not yet part of the domain model."},{"term":"Auto-split","definition":"Proposed automatic decomposition of an overweight Shipment into multiple sub-shipments, each assignable to a different Carrier."},{"term":"Supervisor","definition":"A privileged role that is currently the only authority permitted to reassign a Carrier on a locked Shipment."},{"term":"Carrier Assignment Lock","definition":"The invariant that once a Carrier is assigned to a Shipment, only a Supervisor can change that assignment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state-integrity constraint enforced at dispatch time. Violating it means a Shipment could be dispatched with zero or multiple Carriers, creating accountability and tracking corruption.","testable_assertion":"At any point in the Shipment lifecycle, querying a Shipment's assigned Carriers returns exactly one."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Once a Carrier is assigned to a Shipment, only a Supervisor can reassign it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is an authorization invariant protecting assignment integrity. Without it, any process could silently swap Carriers, breaking auditability and chain-of-custody.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to change Carrier assignment by a non-Supervisor actor is rejected with an authorization error."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The auto-split feature proposes that shipments exceeding 500 kg are automatically split into sub-shipments each assigned to a different carrier without supervisor involvement.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 500 kg threshold is a business policy that could be changed. However, the bypassing of supervisor involvement contradicts BR-2.","testable_assertion":"A shipment weighing >500 kg triggers automatic creation of sub-shipments, each with exactly one carrier, without supervisor approval."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A Shipment weighing over 500 kg is created","when":"The auto-split process executes","then":"The original Shipment is decomposed into N sub-shipments, each weighing ≤500 kg, and each sub-shipment has exactly one assigned Carrier (preserving BR-1)."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A Shipment weighing ≤500 kg is created","when":"The system evaluates for auto-split","then":"No split occurs; the Shipment proceeds with its single assigned Carrier."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An auto-split has occurred and a sub-shipment already has a Carrier assigned","when":"A non-Supervisor actor attempts to reassign the Carrier on that sub-shipment","then":"The reassignment is rejected, preserving BR-2."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A Shipment that has already been assigned a Carrier exceeds 500 kg","when":"Auto-split is triggered","then":"The existing Carrier assignment lock is respected — the split and reassignment requires Supervisor authorization OR the split must occur before initial Carrier assignment."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A Shipment weighs exactly 500 kg","expected_behavior":"No split occurs — the threshold is 'exceeds 500 kg', not 'equals or exceeds'."},{"scenario":"A Shipment already has a locked Carrier assignment when auto-split is triggered","expected_behavior":"Contradiction: auto-split wants to reassign without supervisor, but BR-2 requires supervisor for any reassignment. System must reject or escalate."},{"scenario":"Auto-split produces a sub-shipment that itself exceeds 500 kg (e.g., indivisible cargo)","expected_behavior":"Undefined — the feature request does not address indivisible freight that cannot be split below threshold."},{"scenario":"Parent Shipment is split but one sub-shipment fails Carrier assignment","expected_behavior":"Undefined — must the entire split be rolled back atomically, or can partial assignment exist?"},{"scenario":"Tracking and billing continuity after split","expected_behavior":"Undefined — customers tracking the original Shipment ID need visibility into sub-shipments. Invoice responsibility per sub-shipment is unspecified."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Potential efficiency gains by distributing heavy shipments across multiple carriers, possibly reducing per-carrier surcharges. However, incorrect implementation risks billing errors and disputed invoices across split sub-shipments.","operational":"Introduces a new aggregate relationship (parent/child shipments) that does not exist in the current model. Significant domain model evolution required. Operationally, dispatch coordination becomes more complex with multiple carriers per logical shipment.","user":"Customers may be confused by a single shipment becoming multiple tracking entities. Supervisors lose oversight authority if bypass is allowed, undermining their operational control role.","risk":"HIGH — This feature as described directly violates two production invariants. Shipping it as-is would corrupt the domain model's guarantees. The lack of supervisor involvement removes an audit and safety control. Regulatory implications for chain-of-custody in certain freight categories are unknown.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Supervisors","impact":"Their exclusive reassignment authority is bypassed, reducing operational control and accountability."},{"group":"Carriers","impact":"May receive partial shipments without the usual supervisor-vetted assignment process, affecting load planning."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"A single shipment becomes multiple sub-shipments — tracking, billing, and delivery expectations change."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Must introduce parent-child shipment hierarchy, a fundamental domain model change, not a simple feature addition."},{"group":"Finance/Billing","impact":"Must handle invoicing across split sub-shipments where one logical order maps to multiple carrier assignments."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The feature request explicitly contradicts two established production invariants (one-carrier-per-shipment assignment lock, supervisor-only reassignment). The contradiction is clear and unambiguous — both the existing rules and the new request are explicitly stated and cannot both hold. Confidence is low because the spec cannot be implemented as written without breaking production guarantees.","high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction between auto-split multi-carrier assignment and the one-Carrier-per-Shipment invariant is unambiguous","The bypass of supervisor authority directly violates BR-2","Sub-shipment is a new aggregate concept not present in the current domain model"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the product team intends sub-shipments to be first-class Shipments (which would preserve BR-1 per sub-shipment) or a new entity type","Whether the 500 kg threshold accounts for indivisible cargo","Billing, tracking, and customer notification implications of splitting"],"assumptions":["The existing domain model as described is accurate and enforced in production","The product team's request is as stated with no additional context provided"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature requires assigning multiple carriers to what is logically one shipment and bypassing supervisor authorization for reassignment. This directly contradicts BR-1 (exactly one Carrier per Shipment) and BR-2 (only Supervisors can reassign Carriers). Both the existing invariants and the new requirement are explicitly stated — they cannot coexist as written.","affected_stakeholders":["Supervisors","Carriers","Customers","Engineering","Finance/Billing"],"options":["A: Reject the feature — the existing invariants are sacrosanct and the product team must find another approach (e.g., manual supervisor-approved splitting).","B: Evolve the domain model — introduce a parent-child Shipment hierarchy where each sub-shipment is a first-class Shipment with its own single Carrier, preserving BR-1 per sub-shipment. The parent becomes a logical grouping, not a dispatchable entity. Supervisor approval is still required for the split operation.","C: Evolve the domain model as in B, but allow auto-split without supervisor involvement by defining it as an 'initial assignment' rather than a 'reassignment' — the split creates new sub-shipments that never had a prior Carrier, so BR-2 technically does not apply."],"recommendation":"Option B. Introduce a Shipment hierarchy where sub-shipments are full Shipments (preserving BR-1), but require Supervisor approval for the split operation (preserving BR-2's intent). The 500 kg threshold triggers a split *request* that a Supervisor must approve, after which each sub-shipment gets its own locked Carrier assignment. This preserves both invariants while enabling the business capability. Option C is viable only if the product team confirms that supervisor oversight of multi-carrier distribution is genuinely unnecessary."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"The feature request says 'without requiring supervisor involvement', but the existing model makes Supervisor the sole authority for any change to Carrier assignment. If a Shipment already has a Carrier when auto-split triggers, the system must either violate the supervisor lock or refuse to split — there is no middle ground without a domain model change.","affected_stakeholders":["Supervisors","Engineering"],"options":["Require auto-split to occur only before initial Carrier assignment, making supervisor lock irrelevant.","Require Supervisor approval for the split, contradicting the feature request's 'without supervisor involvement' clause.","Redefine the split as a new domain event that is explicitly exempt from the reassignment lock — requires formal domain model amendment."],"recommendation":"Require the product team to clarify whether auto-split occurs before or after Carrier assignment. If before, the supervisor lock is not violated. If after, Supervisor involvement cannot be avoided without formally amending the invariant."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Auto-split assigns multiple carriers to a single logical shipment","business_rule_broken":"BR-1: A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier"},{"violation":"Auto-split bypasses supervisor authority for carrier assignment changes","business_rule_broken":"BR-2: Only a Supervisor can reassign a Carrier on a locked Shipment"}]}
